title: Stage 10A-10Z · Error prevention and x2 batch quality gates
purpose: Convert repeated development mistakes into repository-owned diagnostics before Pull request merge and before Lovable sync prompts.

batchScale.reason: The user explicitly requested x2 batch size and a strategy to diagnose, fix, and prevent repeated errors before Lovable handoff.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: Existing SysDevicesPage tests failed after adding a new lifecycle endpoint because mocked fetch counts were not updated.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: When a batch adds a frontend data endpoint to an existing page, update tests for request counts and add a named assertion for the new endpoint.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: A shared WorkerMetric type was too narrow for a new string-valued metric.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: Run typecheck before PR creation and widen shared UI metric contracts deliberately when new displayed values are not numeric.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: preflight-all tests needed updates after adding a new stage preflight step.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: Every new preflight script must be added to preflight-all and preflight-all tests in the same commit.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: A dry-run summary directory appeared as untracked var/ output.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: Before staging, run temporary artifact detection for var/, test-results stage outputs, deno.lock, and untracked dry-run summaries.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: Project-memory text initially described the batch as current work instead of confirmed post-merge state.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: Before commit, project-memory must identify the implemented batch as confirmed and the next batch as hypothesis.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: gh GraphQL status calls timed out while PR checks were running.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: When GraphQL checks time out, use gh REST API check-runs and continue waiting rather than sending Lovable prompt early.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: The new guard initially scanned a renderer file that defines forbidden-pattern regexes and produced false positives against its own pattern list.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: Protected-file scans must target product, docs, and manifest surfaces, not helper files that intentionally define forbidden marker patterns.

diagnosedDefects.symptom: The previous Stage 9N-9Z guard still required the original Stage 10A-10L hypothesis marker after the batch expanded to Stage 10A-10Z.
diagnosedDefects.prevention: When expanding a hypothesis into a larger batch, keep the original hypothesis as a historical marker until older guards are intentionally migrated.

preventionRules.rule: Lovable prompt is invalid until the PR is merged into main and local main verifies the merged stage.
The next normal product/process batch after Stage 9N-9Z must target 26 related stages unless a documented hotfix reason applies.
Every repeated defect must be recorded as a diagnosed defect with an explicit prevention rule or guard.
Untracked dry-run outputs, var/, deno.lock, and unexpected report files must be removed or intentionally documented before commit.
Stage-specific preflight scripts must be wired into preflight-all and covered by preflight-all.test in the same PR.
npm run typecheck must pass before creating the PR.
A guard may scan its own required markers, but protected boundary scans must exclude files that define forbidden marker patterns.
Do not remove historical next-stage markers that older guards still verify; mark them as historical when a larger batch supersedes them.

productBoundary.dataVisibility: repository metadata only; no secrets, patient identifiers, tokens, signed URLs, worker payloads, or storage object paths

lovableHandoff.expectedConfirmation: Confirmed: Stage 10A-10Z synced from main, no conflicts.
